ads.title: 1996 Atlanta Olympics Sponsor Pins – Framed, Limited Edition
ads.description: Original 1996 Atlanta Olympic Games Sponsor Pin Collection, professionally framed. This is a limited edition piece (No. 922 of 1200) featuring authentic sponsor pins from the 1996 Summer Olympics in Atlanta. Includes pins from major global brands such as IBM, Visa, AT&T, Delta Air Lines, Motorola, Kodak, BMW, Holiday Inn, Texaco, Swatch, and many more. Original Olympic sponsor pins Limited edition plaque included Beautiful wooden frame with glass Pins are well-preserved and securely mounted Great display piece for collectors, Olympics memorabilia fans, or sports history enthusiasts Condition: Very good overall condition Frame and pins show light age-related wear consistent with vintage items.

ads.author: Spencer Echols
ads.title: 1979 Williams FLASH Pinball Project - Complete Playfield & Backbox
ads.description: Selling a vintage Williams FLASH pinball project. This is a classic Steve Ritchie design and a great candidate for a restoration or a high-end parts donor. Included: Fully Populated Playfield: Original wiring harness, solenoids, and drop target assemblies all intact underneath. Complete Backbox: All original score display boards and master display board included. Original Backglass: Artwork is in great shape with very minimal flaking—vibrant colors. Cabinet Parts: Side art stencils still visible. Selling as-is/untested. Perfect for someone looking to rebuild a legendary game. Local pickup in Arlington / Jacksonville. Bring a truck

ads.title: California Speed Video Driving Arcade Game
ads.description: Up for your consideration is a California Speed Arcade driving game. It plays as is but would definitely benefit from a LCD monitor. I’ve had this machine for about 8 years and it has just started buzzing when you turn it on. I don’t know anything about these but I did talk to an arcade dealer and he said that it’s likely inference or the sound board. This machine does have the ability to be paired with another machine.

ads.author: Jon Sutcliffe
ads.title: Centuri Gyruss Arcade Game
ads.description: This is an original working 1983 Centuri Gyruss arcade game with a 19" CRT. 100% working - No issues - All works as it should This was in an arcade for some of its 43 year history. It's not perfect but pretty nice and very clean. Look at video and pictures, ask for more. If you're local, come to look it over and play a few games! Owner's manual and schematics are in backdoor pocket. Keys and locks for coin door and backdoor. Set to free play but can be configured to accept quarters or tokens. (Quarter coin mechs are included) All Original: - Gryuss PCBs and wiring harnesses - G07-CBO 19"CRT and chassis - Marquee and Bezel - Side art - Joystick New: - Control Panel Overlay art - Buttons - Message me to discuss game details, ask for more pictures, videos. - Prefer local pickup so you can see it and play it. I will help load. - Will ship if you make all shipper arrangements - Can deliver ~locally to your door for a fee. No stairs or other adventures. Make me an offer! Cash or PayPal. No Checks. I will consider trade for a pinball.

ads.author: Dirk Smith Johnson
ads.title: Rare Williams OXO Pinball Machine
ads.description: Very rare Williams OXO pinball machine. Previously featured at the Roanoke Pinball Museum in Virginia. This is a unique electro-mechanical game that plays tic-tac-toe! This game has been in fully working condition until recently when it started blowing a fuse during game restart. It's most likely a switch contact somewhere that's broken, but I've been too busy to diagnose it, and I need the room, so I'm willing to sell it for less than I paid to someone who wants a unique game, and is willing to tinker with it. This game is basically in original condition except for being upgraded with $200+ worth of LEDs to make it brighter and more reliable.
